words.definition: The life story of a person written by themselves.
words.example: His autobiography chronicles a childhood in postwar Vienna.

words.definition: A person who loves or collects books.
words.example: The bibliophile owned first editions of every Dickens novel.

words.definition: A harsh, discordant mixture of sounds.
words.example: The cacophony of car horns made conversation impossible.

words.definition: A political leader who exploits popular prejudices.
words.example: Historians warned that the demagogue thrived on manufactured outrage.

words.definition: Lasting for a very short time.
words.example: Fame in the digital age is often ephemeral.

words.definition: Very attentive to accuracy and detail.
words.example: She was fastidious about grammar in every report.

words.definition: Excessively talkative, especially on trivial matters.
words.example: Their garrulous neighbour cornered them for an hour.

words.definition: A person devoted to the pursuit of pleasure.
words.example: The hedonist saw no virtue in self-denial.

words.definition: A person who attacks cherished beliefs or institutions.
words.example: The iconoclast dismantled the discipline's founding assumptions.

words.definition: Extreme patriotism, especially in the form of aggressive foreign policy.
words.example: The tabloids traded reason for jingoism during the crisis.

words.definition: A person with a compulsive urge to steal.
words.example: Therapists distinguish kleptomaniacs from ordinary thieves.

words.definition: Using very few words.
words.example: His laconic reply left the panel wanting more.

words.definition: A person who dislikes humankind.
words.example: The misanthrope withdrew to a cottage on the moors.

words.definition: A sentimental longing for the past.
words.example: The song stirred a wave of nostalgia for his student years.

words.definition: Knowing everything.
words.example: The novel adopts an omniscient narrator.

words.definition: A solution or remedy for all difficulties.
words.example: Technology is no panacea for social inequality.

words.definition: Extremely idealistic and unrealistic.
words.example: His quixotic scheme to end poverty ignored basic economics.

words.definition: A person who lives in seclusion.
words.example: The elderly recluse had not left her house in decades.

words.definition: A person who flatters others to gain advantage.
words.example: The court was full of sycophants and few advisers.

words.definition: A person who abstains completely from alcohol.
words.example: As a teetotaller, he ordered sparkling water.

words.definition: Present, appearing, or found everywhere.
words.example: Smartphones have become ubiquitous in classrooms.

words.definition: Using more words than are needed.
words.example: The verbose introduction obscured a simple point.

words.definition: A person who fears or hates foreigners.
words.example: The party's rhetoric emboldened xenophobes.

words.definition: An unsophisticated country person.
words.example: He resented being dismissed as a yokel.

words.definition: A person who is fanatical in pursuit of ideals.
words.example: Reformers must not become zealots blind to nuance.

words.definition: One who engages in a pursuit as a pastime rather than a profession.
words.example: The tournament welcomed amateurs and professionals alike.

words.definition: Fluent in two languages.
words.example: Bilingual staff were essential at the border clinic.

words.definition: A person living at the same time as another.
words.example: Newton and Leibniz were contemporaries.

words.definition: A person's first public appearance.
words.example: Her debut on stage drew a standing ovation.

words.definition: A poem of serious reflection, typically a lament for the dead.
words.example: He composed an elegy for his mentor.

words.definition: An exact copy of written or printed material.
words.example: The library holds a facsimile of the original charter.

words.definition: A person who eats to excess.
words.example: Only a glutton would order dessert after that meal.

words.definition: An animal that feeds on plants.
words.example: Deer are pure herbivores.

words.definition: Habitual sleeplessness.
words.example: Chronic insomnia impaired his judgment at work.

words.definition: Relating to young people or characteristic of youth.
words.example: The court reserved juvenile offenders for a separate wing.

words.definition: A blood relative, especially a male one.
words.example: He returned to bury a distant kinsman.

words.definition: The vocabulary of a person, language, or branch of knowledge.
words.example: Legal Latin has entered the mainstream lexicon.

words.definition: A book, document, or piece of music written by hand.
words.example: The medieval manuscript was displayed under glass.

words.definition: A person new to or inexperienced in an activity.
words.example: Even as a novice, she outpaced experienced climbers.

words.definition: A notice of a death, especially in a newspaper.
words.example: The obituary listed his many public services.

words.definition: A person walking along a road or in a developed area.
words.example: The city added crossings to protect pedestrians.

words.definition: The minimum number of members required to conduct business.
words.example: The committee could not vote without a quorum.

words.definition: A general vote by the electorate on a single political question.
words.example: The referendum settled the constitutional question.

words.definition: An act of speaking one's thoughts aloud when alone.
words.example: Hamlet's soliloquy remains a touchstone of the theatre.

words.definition: A pupil who stays away from school without leave.
words.example: Truants were escorted back to the classroom.

words.definition: An imagined place in which everything is perfect.
words.example: Every utopia hides an authoritarian streak.

words.definition: A person who has had long experience in a field.
words.example: Union veterans mentored the new apprentices.

words.definition: A strong desire to travel.
words.example: Her wanderlust took her from Lisbon to Lhasa.

words.definition: A dry photocopying technique.
words.example: Xerography revolutionised office work.

words.definition: An expert in the study of animals.
words.example: The zoologist tagged the migrating herd.

words.definition: One who selflessly helps others.
words.example: The altruist donated most of her salary to charity.

words.definition: Hostile and aggressive.
words.example: His belligerent tone silenced the room.

words.definition: A procession of vehicles or riders.
words.example: A cavalcade of jeeps entered the town.

words.definition: A sudden and ignominious failure.
words.example: The launch turned into a total debacle.

words.definition: Vivacious and enthusiastic.
words.example: Her effervescent laugh lit up the party.

words.definition: Someone who has escaped and is being pursued.
words.example: The fugitive was caught at the border.

words.definition: To manipulate boundaries to favor a party.
words.example: Critics said the map was gerrymandered.

words.definition: A ranked system.
words.example: She rose quickly in the corporate hierarchy.

words.definition: Too great to be expressed in words.
words.example: The view had an ineffable beauty.

words.definition: To throw off as unwanted.
words.example: They jettisoned the plan when costs rose.

words.definition: Praise for an achievement.
words.example: Kudos to the team for finishing early.

words.definition: A person involved in a lawsuit.
words.example: Both litigants appeared in court.

words.definition: A soldier hired for pay.
words.example: The mercenary fought for the highest bidder.

words.definition: A beginner or novice.
words.example: As a neophyte, he asked many questions.

words.definition: A member of a small ruling group.
words.example: The oligarch controlled several industries.

words.definition: A man who has many casual affairs.
words.example: She left him after learning he was a philanderer.

words.definition: A traitor collaborating with the enemy.
words.example: History remembers him as a quisling.

words.definition: A skilled storyteller.
words.example: The raconteur held guests spellbound.

words.definition: A sleepwalker.
words.example: The somnambulist wandered downstairs at night.

words.definition: One who betrays their side.
words.example: The turncoat leaked secrets to rivals.

words.definition: One who takes power by force.
words.example: The usurper seized the throne.

words.definition: One who watches others secretly.
words.example: The voyeur was arrested for spying.

words.definition: A wasteful or good-for-nothing person.
words.example: Her uncle was branded a wastrel.

words.definition: A person attracted to foreign cultures.
words.example: The xenophile collected art from every continent.

words.definition: A soft, gentle breeze.
words.example: A zephyr rustled the curtains.

words.definition: One who opposes all forms of government.
words.example: The anarchist rejected every authority.

words.definition: An official in an administration.
words.example: The bureaucrat processed hundreds of forms.

words.definition: An expert judge in matters of taste.
words.example: She is a connoisseur of French wine.

words.definition: A person with a superficial interest.
words.example: He was a dilettante rather than a scholar.

words.definition: A person who has emigrated for political reasons.
words.example: The émigré wrote memoirs of the old country.

words.definition: An outbreak of public anger.
words.example: The remark caused a furore online.

words.definition: Deliberate killing of a large group.
words.example: The tribunal tried the leaders for genocide.

words.definition: An apparent perception of something not present.
words.example: Dehydration can cause hallucinations.

words.definition: A situation in which no progress is possible.
words.example: Talks reached an impasse.

words.definition: Special words used by a profession.
words.example: Legal jargon confused the jury.

words.definition: A constantly changing pattern.
words.example: Autumn painted a kaleidoscope of colours.

words.definition: Humour or lack of seriousness.
words.example: A moment of levity relieved the tension.

words.definition: A stately tomb.
words.example: The mausoleum houses royal remains.

words.definition: A large ancient cemetery.
words.example: Archaeologists mapped the necropolis.

words.definition: A skilled public speaker.
words.example: The orator moved the crowd to tears.

words.definition: A seemingly contradictory statement.
words.example: 'Less is more' is a classic paradox.

words.definition: A state of uncertainty.
words.example: I was in a quandary about the offer.

words.definition: A person who deserts a cause.
words.example: The renegade joined the opposition.

words.definition: A situation with no progress possible.
words.example: Negotiations ended in stalemate.

words.definition: A long, angry speech.
words.example: His tirade lasted twenty minutes.

words.definition: Offence or annoyance.
words.example: She took umbrage at the comment.

words.definition: A trace of something no longer present.
words.example: Only vestiges of the old wall remain.

words.definition: A person who travels aimlessly.
words.example: The wanderer had no fixed address.

words.definition: A rock fragment enclosed in another rock.
words.example: Geologists studied the xenolith.

words.definition: A standard used for comparison.
words.example: Profit is not the only yardstick of success.
